title: 1.1 Identifies the task scheduling system used by paperless-ngx (e.g., django-q, Celery Beat, APScheduler, RQ)
1.2 Lists all the periodic tasks that are registered (e.g., sanity check task, classifier training task, index optimization task, mail processing task)
1.3 States the execution frequency for the sanity check task (e.g., weekly, every 7 days, once per week)
1.4 States the execution frequency for the classifier training task (e.g., hourly, every 60 minutes, once per hour)
1.5 States the execution frequency for the index optimization task (e.g., daily, every 24 hours, once per day)
1.6 States the execution frequency for the mail processing task (e.g., every 10 minutes, 10-minute interval, 600 seconds)
1.7 Identifies where periodic task schedules are configured (e.g., database migrations, migration files like 1004_sanity_check_schedule.py)
1.8 Reports what the sanity checker validates (e.g., thumbnail files, original document files, file checksums, orphaned files)
1.9 Describes the log output produced by the sanity checker (e.g., error messages, warning messages, info messages, success confirmation)
1.10 Identifies a database table that tracks task execution history (e.g., django_q_task table, task history table)
1.11 Describes what happens when a scheduled task fails (e.g., retry logic exists, unlimited retries, retry timeout configuration)
1.12 Distinguishes between tasks that run on startup versus tasks that run on schedule (e.g., signal handlers at startup, periodic tasks on intervals)
1.13 Identifies where periodic task functions are defined in the codebase (e.g., documents.tasks module, paperless_mail.tasks module)
1.14 Addresses whether automatic database cleanup exists (e.g., confirms cleanup task exists, states no dedicated cleanup task exists)
1.15 Describes what controls whether maintenance tasks can be enabled (e.g., admin interface, database modification, environment variables, configuration settings)
2.1: Claims scheduled tasks run immediately on startup when catch_up is False
2.2: States there is no task execution history tracking in the database
